old_claim.note: Formal Logic (Syllogism) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Formal Logic (Syllogism) is a historical predecessor for symbolic, rule-based reasoning systems.

source_shape.source_locator: Stanford entry section on Logic and AI ties symbolic AI and expert systems to historical logic traditions, including syllogistic reasoning as an intellectual predecessor, without presenting logic as a direct implementation requirement.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The entry frames logic-based AI as a historical lineage from formal logic traditions to AI representation and inference methods.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports interpreting formal logic as antecedent lineage rather than a hard enabling component because expert systems rely on logic-as-antecedent ideas but can be introduced through multiple implementation traditions.

ontology_before: formal_logic_syllogism was modeled as an enabling prerequisite for Symbolic AI & Expert Systems.
ontology_after: formal_logic_syllogism is now modeled as a historical predecessor relationship instead of an enabling component.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: the dependency is kept and the graph still indicates the historical path. Changed: edge semantics now encode lineage rather than enabling.
would_reject_if: A primary source showed that modern expert systems cannot be engineered without symbolic syllogistic formalisms.
short_reason: Logic is the field ancestor for symbolic reasoning, not a direct required enabling substrate.
